series_metadata.series_description: The Project Management Artifacts describe the documentation that aids in the management of a project. Examples of project management artifacts include but are not limited to project definition, scope, communications, risk, cost, issues, quality, status, lessons learned, project human resources and procurement, project charters, scope statements, project estimates and project actuals, project plans, and related correspondence.

retention_rules.disposition: Electronic: Retain 10 years from the end of the contract date for large projects, 10 years from project completion or cancellation date for reoccurring projects, retain 5 years from project completion or cancellation date for medium non-reoccurring projects, retain 3 years from project completion or cancellation date for small non-reoccurring projects. ; Paper: If scanned, verify image and securely destroy. Otherwise retain 10 years from the end of the contract date for large projects, 10 years from project completion or cancellation date for reoccurring projects, retain 5 years from project completion or cancellation date for medium non-reoccurring projects, retain 3 years from project completion or cancellation date for small non-reoccurring projects., Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred
